verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone retained by the KDEL motif. However, substantial evidence documents its translocation to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in multiple non-baseline contexts: (1) cancer/oncogenic state — GRP78 is well-established as a cancer cell-surface protein detected by surface biotinylation, flow cytometry, and IHC on tumor cells across many solid tumor types, driving drug-targeting programs including antibody-drug conjugates and bispecifics; (2) cell stress (ER stress, hypoxia, oxidative stress) induces surface GRP78 on non-tumor cells; (3) apoptosis/ICD — surface GRP78 is a hallmark of immunogenic cell death; (4) viral infection — some viruses exploit surface GRP78 as a co-receptor. The surface pool is wash-resistant and is the basis of multiple clinical-stage cancer therapeutic programs. Baseline localization is ER-luminal, but the cancer-cell ecto-pool is well-documented and therapeutically exploited.
